implementation_notes: Implementation via task-work delegation. Files planned: 0, Files actual: 0
requirements_addressed: AC-001: factory returns (PipelinePublisher, PipelineLifecycleEmitter) bound to supplied client
AC-002: no second nats.connect is opened by the factory
AC-003: on_transition exists and dispatches per lifecycle literal (verified)
AC-004: publish failure logs at WARNING and is swallowed by emitter._safe_publish (existing behaviour, factory wires it into production path)
AC-005: ruff check + ruff format pass with zero errors

completion_promises.criterion_text: build_publisher_and_emitter(client) returns a (PipelinePublisher, PipelineLifecycleEmitter) tuple bound to the supplied client.
completion_promises.evidence: Implemented build_publisher_and_emitter in src/forge/cli/_serve_deps_lifecycle.py. The factory constructs PipelinePublisher(client) and then PipelineLifecycleEmitter(publisher=publisher, config=...) and returns the tuple. Tests TestFactoryReturnsBoundPair::test_returns_publisher_and_emitter_types, ::test_publisher_is_bound_to_supplied_client (asserts publisher._nc is fake_client), and ::test_emitter_is_bound_to_returned_publisher (asserts emitter._publisher is publisher) verify the binding.

completion_promises.criterion_text: No second NATS connection is opened anywhere in this factory.
completion_promises.evidence: The factory only calls PipelinePublisher(client) — it never imports nats nor calls nats.connect. Test TestFactoryDoesNotOpenSecondConnection::test_no_call_to_nats_connect patches sys.modules['nats'] with a sentinel whose connect is an AsyncMock that raises AssertionError on call, then asserts connect was never called. test_publisher_publish_uses_supplied_client confirms publish writes go through the supplied client, not a phantom second connection.

completion_promises.criterion_text: emitter.on_transition(new_state) exists and dispatches to the correct emit_* method for every lifecycle literal in DDR-006.
completion_promises.evidence: PipelineLifecycleEmitter.on_transition already exists in src/forge/pipeline/__init__.py (lines 594-632) with the TRANSITION_TO_EMITTER dispatch table covering all six declared transitions: PREPARING->RUNNING (emit_started), RUNNING->PAUSED (emit_paused), PAUSED->RUNNING (emit_resumed), FINALISING->COMPLETE (emit_complete), *->FAILED (emit_failed), *->CANCELLED (emit_cancelled). Per the task brief: 'If on_transition already exists, this task verifies it.' Verified by TestOnTransitionDispatchMatrix with 7 parametrised cases including both wildcard FAILED and CANCELLED branches.

completion_promises.criterion_text: Publish failure on any emit_* does not raise to callers — it logs at WARNING and returns (DDR-007 Failure-mode contract, ADR-ARCH-008).
completion_promises.evidence: The contract is satisfied by the existing PipelinePublisher._publish_envelope (logs at WARNING via logger.warning at line 193 of pipeline_publisher.py before raising PublishFailure) combined with PipelineLifecycleEmitter._safe_publish (catches PublishFailure and returns without re-raising at lines 748-757 of pipeline/__init__.py). The factory wires both objects together so this swallow-and-log path is the production code path. Existing test TestPublishFailureSwallowed::test_every_emit_method_swallows_publish_failure in tests/forge/test_pipeline_lifecycle.py verifies all eight emit_* methods do not raise on PublishFailure. Per the task brief: 'The PipelinePublisher class itself is not modified. Just construct it.' so no further code change is required for this AC.

completion_promises.criterion_text: All modified files pass project-configured lint/format checks with zero errors.
completion_promises.evidence: Ran 'ruff check src/forge/cli/_serve_deps_lifecycle.py tests/forge/test_lifecycle_factory.py' — output: 'All checks passed!'. Ran 'ruff format src/forge/cli/_serve_deps_lifecycle.py tests/forge/test_lifecycle_factory.py' — both files now formatted to project conventions. Both files compile cleanly via python -m py_compile.
